steps.title: Identify the chromium comparison
steps.content: The passage establishes that ferritic stainless steel contains at least 10.5% chromium and more chromium than austenitic stainless steel. Since ferritic's minimum is 10.5% and austenitic is always less than ferritic, austenitic must be below 10.5%.

steps.title: Connect to the applications
steps.content:
Since washing machines use 200 series (austenitic) and furnaces use 300 series (austenitic), both have less than 10.5% chromium—exactly what choice A states.

  • B reverses the nickel relationship
  • C falsely denies the cubic structure to storage containers
  • D incorrectly equates nitrogen levels between austenitic and ferritic types
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
